folio.label: 89r
folio.l: If it be good , in Thomas lyf of Ynde
ñe lyn here ful of anger , and of Ire
Witú whicú the deuele , settetú ©oure hert a fyre
And chiden heere , the holyselyholy sely Innocentsely275 This word is added after a caret mark in the right margin.Est
ñoure wyfwyf , that is so meke and pacient
And therfore Thoms , trowe me if æè lest
Ne stryue nat witú thy wyf , as for the best
And bere this word a wey , now be thy feytú
Touchyngö swicú thyngö , lo what the wyseman seitú
Witú Inne thyn hous , ne be æè no lyou~
To thyne suggettz , do non oppressiou~
Ne make thyn acqueyntaunce , nat to flee
And Thomas , ©et eftsones charge I the
Be war from hire , that in thy bosom slepetú
War froo the serpent , that so slyly crepetú
Vnder the gras , and styngetú sotilly
Be war my sone , and herkene paciently
That twenty thousand men , han lost here lyues
For stryuyngö witú here lemmans , and here wyues
Now syn ©e han , so holy meeke a wyf´
What nedetú ©ow , Thomas to maken strif
Ther nys I wysse , no serpent so cruelle
Whan a man tret vp on his tail , ne half so felle
As womman is , whan she hatú caugút an Ire
Vengeaunce is thanne , al that they desire
Ire is a synne onon , of the greete ofof Seuene
Abhominable , to the god of heuene
And to hi~ self , it is destruccione
This euäy lewed vyker , or Persone
Can seyn , how Ire , engendretú homycide
Ire is in sotú , executour of pryde
I coude of Ire , seyn so mechil sorwe
My tale shuld laste , til to morwe
And æäfore preye I god , bothe day & nygút
An Irous man , god sende hi~ litel mygút
It is gret harm , and certes greet pitee
To sette an Irous man , in heye degree
¢ Whilom there was , an Irous potestate

note.notetxt: 309There is a cross in dry point in the right margin.ODR

folio.l: As seitú Senek , that duryngö his estate
Vp on a day , out redyn knygútes twoo
And as fortune wolde , that it was so
That oon of hem cam hom , that othir nougút
A noon the knygút , byfore the Iuge is brougút
That seide thus , thow hast thy felawe slayne
For whicú I deme the , to the detú certayne
And to a nother knygút , comaunded he
